commands.name: echo

patterns.explanation: Pads with leading zeros so all numbers have equal width

commands.name: sleep
commands.description: Delay for a specified amount of time

patterns.explanation: Waits for 5 seconds before continuing

patterns.explanation: Pauses execution for 2 minutes

patterns.explanation: Pauses for 500 milliseconds

patterns.text: delay between retries in a loop
patterns.command: while ! curl -s http://localhost:8080; do sleep 3; done
patterns.explanation: Retries a connection every 3 seconds until successful

commands.name: true
commands.description: Do nothing, successfully (return exit code 0)

patterns.text: create an infinite loop
patterns.command: while true; do echo "running"; sleep 1; done
patterns.explanation: The loop condition always succeeds, running forever

patterns.text: use true as a no-op placeholder
patterns.command: if [ -f config ]; then true; else echo "missing"; fi
patterns.explanation: Acts as a do-nothing command in the then branch

patterns.text: override a command to always succeed
patterns.explanation: Ensures the pipeline succeeds even if some_command fails

patterns.text: create an empty file using redirection
patterns.explanation: Creates or truncates a file to zero bytes

commands.name: false
commands.description: Do nothing, unsuccessfully (return exit code 1)

patterns.text: force a script to fail at a certain point
patterns.explanation: Returns a non-zero exit code to signal failure

patterns.text: test error handling in a pipeline
patterns.explanation: The second command is skipped because false returns failure

patterns.text: use as a disabled loop condition
patterns.command: while false; do echo "never runs"; done
patterns.explanation: The loop body never executes

patterns.text: trigger an ERR trap for testing
patterns.explanation: Deliberately triggers the error trap

commands.name: claude
commands.description: Claude Code CLI - run an AI coding assistant non-interactively with -p/--print to pipe its output into shell commands

patterns.text: let claude write the commit message and commit staged changes
patterns.command: git commit -m "$(claude -p 'Review the staged changes with git diff --cached and write a concise conventional commit message. Output only the message text, no markdown, no explanation.')"
patterns.explanation: Runs claude in print mode; it inspects the staged diff and returns a commit message that is substituted into git commit -m

patterns.text: claude commit - AI generated commit message for staged files
patterns.command: git commit -m "$(claude -p 'Review the staged changes with git diff --cached and write a concise conventional commit message. Output only the commit message text, no markdown, no explanation.')"
patterns.explanation: One-liner that asks claude to read the staged diff and produce a commit message, then commits with it

patterns.text: generate a commit message from the staged diff using claude
patterns.command: git diff --cached | claude -p 'Write a concise conventional commit message for this diff. Output only the commit message text, no markdown, no explanation.'
patterns.explanation: Pipes the staged diff into claude, which returns just the commit message on stdout

patterns.text: stage everything and let claude commit it
patterns.command: git add -A && git commit -m "$(claude -p 'Review the staged changes with git diff --cached and write a concise conventional commit message. Output only the commit message text, no markdown, no explanation.')"
patterns.explanation: Stages all changes then asks claude to write the commit message before committing

patterns.text: ask claude a quick question from the shell without starting a session
patterns.explanation: Runs claude non-interactively, prints the response, and exits — ideal for piping or command substitution

patterns.text: pipe a file into claude and get a response back on stdout
patterns.command: cat file.txt | claude -p 'summarize this file in 3 bullet points'
patterns.explanation: Sends file contents as context to claude in print mode and captures the reply

patterns.text: continue the most recent claude conversation in this directory
patterns.explanation: Resumes the last session so previous context is preserved
